meta.title: Academic Programmes — Aletheia Resource Center
meta.description: Explore the academic programmes at Aletheia Resource Center — a rigorous curriculum balanced with co-curricular opportunities that nurture the whole student.

hero.subtitle: From primary through Cambridge IGCSE, our curriculum — grounded in the Ignitia programme through Grade 9 and culminating in Cambridge IGCSE qualifications — nurtures critical thinkers, confident communicators, and principled leaders ready for a rapidly changing world.

overview.heading: A Complete Education Journey
overview.intro: Three connected programmes forming a seamless pathway — Ignitia for Grades 3–9 and Cambridge IGCSE for Grades 10–11 — from a child's first school years through to internationally recognised IGCSE qualifications.
overview.comparisonHeading: At a glance — how the programmes compare

comparisonRows.label: Key Focus
cells: Biblical worldview, literacy, numeracy & inquiry foundations
Core academics mastery through Ignitia Grades 7–9
Cambridge IGCSE subject breadth & internationally recognised qualifications

programmes.title: Primary Programme
programmes.description: A strong foundation in biblical understanding, literacy, numeracy, and scientific inquiry through the Ignitia curriculum — building confident, curious learners across five integrated core subjects.

deepDive.heading: What Each Programme Offers
deepDive.intro: Each stage of learning is carefully designed to build on the last, ensuring every student arrives at their next chapter prepared and confident.

programmes.title: Primary Programme
programmes.qualificationNote: Ignitia is an interactive, mastery-based online curriculum by Alpha Omega Publications, used by the school across Grades 3–6 in five core subject areas with a biblically-integrated approach.
programmes.overview: Our Primary Programme is built on the Ignitia curriculum by Alpha Omega Publications — a mastery-based programme that builds strong academic foundations across five core subjects: Bible, History & Geography, Language Arts, Mathematics, and Science. Students explore the world through structured lessons, hands-on experiments, and biblically-integrated content that nurtures both intellectual curiosity and godly character.
programmes.imageAlt: Young students engaged in hands-on learning activities in a bright classroom

subjects.name: Bible
subjects.desc: A developmental study of the Old and New Testaments across five content strands — biblical literature, biblical background, Christian growth, Christian evidences, and life application. Students explore the lives of Jesus, Paul, Joseph, and other key figures; study Bible archaeology, the Psalms, and Old Testament geography; and grow in Christian character and service.

subjects.name: History & Geography
subjects.desc: Early grades explore US geography, regional communities, and map skills (latitude, longitude, compass rose). By Grade 6, students survey world geography and ancient civilisations — Mesopotamia, Egypt, Israel, Greece, Rome, and the Middle Ages — building historical thinking and cultural awareness.

subjects.name: Language Arts
subjects.desc: A sequential programme building reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills. Students advance from phonics and grammar foundations to literary comprehension, composition, and oral communication — exploring fiction, poetry, biography, and biblical literature. Writing skills progress from sentence-level mechanics to essays, summaries, and short stories.

subjects.name: Mathematics
subjects.desc: A rigorous progression from number sense and arithmetic operations through fractions, decimals, geometry, early algebraic thinking, data analysis, and probability. By Grade 6, students work with ratios, proportions, coordinate planes, and introductory algebraic expressions — with real-world problem-solving integrated at every level.

subjects.name: Science
subjects.desc: A broad, inquiry-driven survey of the natural world covering the human body, plants, animals, health and nutrition, matter, sound, earth science, heat energy, and introductory chemistry and physics. Units include hands-on experiments, observation projects, and scientific inquiry activities that build curiosity and a subject-matter knowledge base.

outcomes: Strong literacy, numeracy, and scientific foundations
Biblical worldview and character integrated across all subjects
Inquisitive, self-directed learners with research and writing skills

programmes.title: Secondary Programme
programmes.qualificationNote: Students complete the Ignitia upper-level programme through Grade 9, building rigorous academic foundations across five core subjects through a mastery-based, biblically-integrated approach before progressing to the Cambridge IGCSE programme.
programmes.overview: Our Secondary Programme advances students through upper-level and named Ignitia courses across Grades 7 to 9 — developing rigorous analytical, writing, and quantitative skills across all five core subjects. Each grade deepens subject knowledge and builds the academic discipline and critical thinking skills that prepare students for the internationally recognised Cambridge IGCSE programme.
programmes.imageAlt: Secondary students working in a well-equipped science laboratory

subjects.name: Bible (Grades 7–9)
subjects.desc: Students complete systematic New and Old Testament surveys, developing a coherent biblical worldview through the study of Christian theology, church history, evidences for the Christian faith, and practical Christian living — applied across academic disciplines.

subjects.name: History & Geography (Grades 7–9)
subjects.desc: Grade 7 surveys the full social sciences spectrum — history methodology, world geography, anthropology, sociology, economics, and political science. Grade 8 is a comprehensive study of American History from European exploration to the present, with emphasis on colonisation, the Civil War, industrialisation, and modern America.

subjects.name: English Language Arts (Grades 7–9)
subjects.desc: Students advance from Language Arts 700–800 to English I and II, engaging with literary fiction, nonfiction, poetry, and drama. Course content includes grammar and sentence structure mastery, vocabulary development through Greek and Latin roots, and production of essays, literary critiques, and research papers — with readings from well-known American and British authors.

subjects.name: Mathematics (Grades 7–9)
subjects.desc: Grade 7 consolidates pre-algebra skills — integers, fractions, proportional reasoning, equations, data analysis, and geometry. Grades 8–9 advance through Algebra I Fundamentals and Algebra I, covering linear equations, functions, inequalities, polynomials, quadratic expressions, and probability and statistics.

subjects.name: Science (Grades 7–9)
subjects.desc: General Science I (Grade 7) covers the scientific method, astronomy, weather, climate, and human anatomy. General Science II (Grade 8) advances into matter, energy, electricity, magnetism, and ecology. Grade 9 students proceed to Integrated Physics & Chemistry or Biology — covering taxonomy, cellular biology, genetics, chemistry of life, human anatomy, and ecological systems.

programmes.subjectNote: In addition to the five core subjects, the Ignitia curriculum offers a range of elective courses for secondary students — including Computer Science, French, Spanish, Music Appreciation, Music Theory, Psychology, Personal Financial Literacy, Physical Education, and more — allowing students to pursue personal interests and develop a well-rounded academic portfolio.
outcomes: Mastery of five core subjects through Grades 7–9 via Ignitia
Strong analytical thinking, writing, and quantitative reasoning
Biblical worldview applied across all academic disciplines
Solid academic foundation for Cambridge IGCSE progression

programmes.title: Cambridge IGCSE Programme
programmes.qualification: Cambridge IGCSE — Cambridge Assessment International Education
programmes.qualificationNote: Cambridge IGCSE is one of the most widely recognised international qualifications for students aged 14–16, accepted by universities and employers in over 160 countries. It provides a thorough grounding in subject content and promotes intellectual engagement.
programmes.overview: Our Cambridge IGCSE Programme is a focused two-year track across Grades 10 and 11, offering students a broad choice of subjects across Sciences, Mathematics, Languages, and Humanities. Students develop strong subject knowledge, critical thinking, and examination skills — earning internationally recognised qualifications that open doors to further education worldwide.
programmes.imageAlt: IGCSE students engaged in focused academic study

subjects.name: Sciences
subjects.desc: Students may choose from Biology, Chemistry, and Physics — covering core theoretical knowledge alongside practical and experimental skills. Each subject builds a rigorous scientific foundation and develops analytical thinking.

subjects.name: Mathematics
subjects.desc: Cambridge IGCSE Mathematics covers number, algebra, geometry, trigonometry, statistics, and probability — developing strong quantitative reasoning and problem-solving skills essential for further study.

subjects.name: English Language & Literature
subjects.desc: English Language develops reading, writing, and communication skills for academic and professional contexts. English Literature engages students with a range of texts — fiction, poetry, and drama — building critical interpretation and essay writing skills.

subjects.name: Humanities
subjects.desc: Students may choose from History, Geography, and Economics — exploring the forces that shape human societies, environments, and economies through evidence-based inquiry and analytical writing.

subjects.name: Modern Languages
subjects.desc: IGCSE language courses develop reading, writing, listening, and speaking proficiency — providing internationally recognised language qualifications that enhance academic and career prospects.

subjects.name: Additional Electives
subjects.desc: A range of additional IGCSE subjects may be offered depending on student interest and availability, allowing students to tailor their subject combination to personal strengths and aspirations.

outcomes: Cambridge IGCSE qualifications across multiple subjects
Internationally recognised credentials accepted in 160+ countries
Strong subject knowledge, critical thinking, and examination readiness
Solid preparation for further education and career pathways

whyChoose.heading: The Aletheia Difference
whyChoose.intro: We don't just teach — we shape the whole child. Here is what sets our programmes apart from the rest.

features.title: Proven, Structured Curriculum
features.body: Primary and secondary students follow the Ignitia mastery-based programme by Alpha Omega Publications through Grade 9. From Grade 10, students complete the globally recognised Cambridge IGCSE framework — earning qualifications accepted by universities and employers in over 160 countries.

features.title: Small Class Sizes
features.body: With intentionally small class sizes, every student receives focused attention and personalised guidance — never just a face in the crowd.

features.title: Experienced Educators
features.body: Our faculty comprises qualified, passionate teachers with deep subject expertise and a genuine commitment to each student's growth and wellbeing.

features.title: Holistic Co-Curricular
features.body: From orchestra and debate to robotics and competitive sports, our rich CCA programme ensures every child discovers and develops their unique talents.

features.title: IGCSE Examination Guidance
features.body: Our experienced teachers provide dedicated support throughout the Cambridge IGCSE examination process — from subject selection and coursework guidance to examination preparation and results review.

features.title: Pastoral Care & Character
features.body: Every student is known by name. Our pastoral system ensures emotional wellbeing, character formation, and a strong sense of belonging throughout their school journey.

enquiryCta.body: Our admissions team is here to answer your questions, arrange a campus visit, and guide you through every step of the enrolment process. We would love to hear from you.
trust: Our team replies within one working day
